assertions.assertion: Output calls AddClientCredentialsTokenManagement() to register the token management services
assertions.rationale: Well-known Duende.AccessTokenManagement API from online docs

assertions.assertion: Output calls AddClient() with configuration including TokenEndpoint, ClientId, ClientSecret, and Scope
assertions.rationale: AddClient configuration is documented in getting started guides

assertions.assertion: Output registers an HTTP client using AddClientCredentialsHttpClient or AddClientCredentialsTokenHandler with the client name
assertions.rationale: AddClientCredentialsHttpClient is a known API from the library

assertions.assertion: Output shows a BackgroundService or worker class that uses IHttpClientFactory.CreateClient to make API calls without manual token code
assertions.rationale: The specific BackgroundService + IHttpClientFactory pattern may not be shown consistently without the skill doc

assertions.assertion: Output uses v4 strongly-typed ClientCredentialsClientName.Parse() for the client name (not a raw string)
assertions.rationale: ClientCredentialsClientName.Parse() is a v4-specific type not widely known; most would use raw strings (v3 pattern)
